question: During standby recovery, if settings are reloaded and primary connection inputs change while the receiver side is already active, what is the internal receiver state label used to mark a reconnect path instead of a first start or a full stop path?

During a team packing job into numbered bins, when repeated reshuffling still leaves almost everything in one family and another split could exceed safe size limits, what internal state label means no more resizing rounds are allowed?

During btree page growth, a freshly extended page can momentarily be blank before it is initialized, which creates a race with maintenance scanning. Which read mode is used so the page is not read from disk and is handed back already locked for safe first initialization?
When someone tries to attach a new uniqueness promise to a data sheet by reusing an old lock, but that lock is already tied to another promise, which all caps internal failure label is raised?

In the startup to login handoff, when a client reaches authentication but matches zero host based access records, which hidden internal authentication method marker is assigned before the no entry rejection path runs?

During standby replay, a parameter change can make logical decoding no longer valid and trigger slot invalidation; if the conflicting slot is still owned, startup uses a recovery conflict interrupt path instead of a plain termination signal. What exact internal reason name is sent for that conflict?

When the wraparound failsafe builds its transaction age cutoff and that cutoff is not a normal transaction id, which named baseline transaction id constant does it fall back to before comparing table age?

A client opens a no-edit transaction and asks to delay its first view until risky neighbors clear out. During that brief parked phase while it waits, what exact internal flag token marks that parked state?

When a user deliberately links an otherwise standalone database object to an add-on package so removing that package later also removes the linked object, while keeping it out of the package's core membership list, which dependency flavor token represents that opt-in link?

A lookup filter asks whether each row's collection covers every item in a request basket. When that request basket is actually empty, the condition becomes broadly true, but the bitmap walk still needs a synthetic driver term if that attribute has no positive term at all. Which internal category marker is injected for that synthetic term?

During a client-driven bulk load over the live connection, the sender accidentally injects the tiny packet whose only purpose is to demand immediate outbound drain in the middle of row payload traffic. The receiver explicitly tolerates that packet and keeps ingesting rows instead of aborting the transfer. What exact internal request token names that tolerated packet?

When someone imports a saved "who-is-still-active" view at the very start of a session, the file can be structurally valid but still rejected because one boundary number is in the tiny reserved ID range used before normal work begins. In another branch, if the child list was too large to keep, the system walks upward through parent links before checking activity. What exact internal threshold name marks the first ID treated as normal work?

When a big ledger is divided into many drawers and one drawer is being peeled away in a two-stage operation, the engine enforces three simultaneous rules: only one drawer can be in that halfway state, drawer-roster reads may skip it once stage one is visible to the reader, and lineage walks treat that drawer as no longer on the family chain. What is the exact yes/no column name in the linkage catalog row that marks this halfway state?

A backend waits for a helper process to report that it has started, but while waiting, the cluster supervisor disappears, so no further lifecycle notices can arrive. Which status value is returned to indicate that this wait can no longer resolve normally?

During a locked-channel network sign-in, a rulebook line demands both a secret check and an identity-document check. The operator can compare either a short label or the document's full identity line; when they choose the full line and mapping misses, what internal selector value marks that choice?

During the final shutdown lap of log streaming, the sender may still pause even after its own durable bytes are available, because nominated downstream partners have not yet proven they durably stored the handoff checkpoint needed for takeover continuity. Which internal wait event token names that pause?

In a two-person change relay on the receiving side, repeated mailbox backpressure makes the coordinator spill the rest of one transaction into side files. After spill-writing finishes, there is an intermediate shared flag set for one loop pass before the helper is allowed to replay those side files, because mailbox messages may still remain. What is the exact internal token for that in-between flag?

When the key-to-drawer directory grows and one crowded drawer is divided into two, some moved records can still leave stale copies in the original drawer. If that original drawer cannot be tidied right away because extra hands are still holding it, the engine leaves a one-bit reminder on it so a later maintenance pass must remove those leftovers before future growth from that same drawer. What is the exact reminder-bit constant name?
During a janitor pass, a zone summary card can represent a zone with zero surviving entries. What exact internal flag name is flipped to encode that "no occupants" zone state?

In the SQL terminal, an operator tries to rewire a built-in ticket dispenser that was auto-attached to a table field during setup. The system can still discover where that dispenser is linked, but it rejects changing the link when that bond belongs to the stricter system-managed class. What is the exact dependency tag used for that stricter class?

During a bundle-upgrade work session, a throwaway object stamped as belonging to that same bundle can be removed without first detaching it; outside that session, the same stamp makes removal climb to the owner container instead of deleting the piece directly. What is the exact relationship-stamp token for that membership link?

During an in-server routine that tries to cross a ledger boundary, the engine first attempts to preserve any hand-held row stream so it can survive the boundary. If that stream comes from a shape that might still mutate data, the preservation path is rejected before spill-space persistence begins. Which internal strategy label must already be present for that preservation conversion to be allowed?

A downstream mirror is configured to eventually support split-signature commits, but at enrollment time it has zero tracked tables. Later, operators widen the upstream feed to include additional tables and then ask the downstream side to do a full backfill pass for those newcomers (not a metadata-only refresh). Which internal tri-state catalog token must still represent that mirror profile during this window, before the background catch-up workers finish and the mode is promoted?

A coordinator parks a named work packet, leaves it around long enough for housekeeping to move its dossier out of the rolling journal and into shelf storage, then later issues the one-shot "finish it now" order. At the moment the server blocks to open and read that shelf copy back, which exact internal wait label is raised?

During a mass "copy the remote aisle map into local stand-ins" operation, the destination and access rights are valid, and the adapter's doorway function exists, but that adapter does not provide the inventory-listing hook required for this operation type. Which internal SQLSTATE macro is emitted on that failure path?
